records.description: King's names Microsoft Copilot as a generative AI text tool available through KCL Microsoft login, subject to academic-integrity and assessment-level rules.
records.howToObtain: Log in with KCL Microsoft credentials; King's says students should use their KCL account.

evidence.sourceTitle: Generative AI: student guidance | King's College London
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Microsoft Copilot is listed under text generation, and the page says it is available to all King's students with KCL Microsoft login credentials.

evidence.sourceTitle: Micro-level: GTAs/teachers/lecturers/PhD Supervisors | King's College London
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Staff are told to try Microsoft Copilot and to log into their KCL account for protected search and increased data security.

evidence.sourceTitle: Generative AI: Guidance for doctoral students, supervisors and examiners | King's College London
evidence.evidenceSnippet: King's says Copilot used while logged into a King's Microsoft account will not store or use inputs in that way, reducing some privacy risks.

records.description: King's explicitly names ChatGPT as a generative AI tool students and staff may use within local assessment rules and acknowledgement requirements.
records.howToObtain: Use the public ChatGPT service; King's does not describe a university procurement path.

evidence.sourceTitle: Generative AI: student guidance | King's College London
evidence.evidenceSnippet: The student guidance lists ChatGPT as a text-generation tool and says King's does not ban any type of AI.

evidence.sourceTitle: Micro-level: GTAs/teachers/lecturers/PhD Supervisors | King's College London
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Staff are advised to experiment with free versions such as ChatGPT, alongside Gemini and Claude AI.

evidence.sourceTitle: Macro-level guidance: University-wide principles and policy | King's College London
evidence.evidenceSnippet: King's warns staff not to use unsupported tools such as ChatGPT to scrutinise student work without explicit permission.

records.description: King's explicitly names Google Gemini as a generative AI tool that may be used within assessment guidance and academic-integrity expectations.
records.howToObtain: Use the public Google Gemini service; King's references free versions rather than a campus license.

evidence.sourceTitle: Generative AI: student guidance | King's College London
evidence.evidenceSnippet: The student guidance lists Google Gemini among text-generation tools and says King's does not ban AI use.

evidence.sourceTitle: Micro-level: GTAs/teachers/lecturers/PhD Supervisors | King's College London
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Staff are told to experiment with free versions such as Google Gemini, ChatGPT, and Claude AI.

evidence.sourceTitle: Macro-level guidance: University-wide principles and policy | King's College London
evidence.evidenceSnippet: King's expects students to acknowledge use of tools such as Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, or ChatGPT.

records.description: King's explicitly names Claude AI alongside mainstream generative AI tools; use is framed as permissible but subject to teaching, assessment, and integrity constraints.
records.howToObtain: Use the public Claude service; King's references free versions and does not mention institutional procurement.

evidence.sourceTitle: Micro-level: GTAs/teachers/lecturers/PhD Supervisors | King's College London
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Staff are advised to experiment with free versions such as Google Gemini, ChatGPT, and Claude AI.

evidence.sourceTitle: Meso-level guidance: departments, programmes and modules | King's College London
evidence.evidenceSnippet: King's says tools such as ChatGPT, Google Gemini, Microsoft Copilot, and Claude AI were not developed by higher education providers.

records.description: ANU officially supports Microsoft Copilot as a generative AI chatbot/productivity assistant for drafting, summarising, generating insights, and image/text tasks.
records.howToObtain: Sign in with an ANU account via the Microsoft 365 Applications Portal or directly at copilot.microsoft.com; ANU also points users to ANUConnect for support.
records.costToUser: Free to ANU staff and students when accessed with an ANU account.

evidence.sourceTitle: Microsoft Copilot AI | Services
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Microsoft Copilot is supported at ANU.

evidence.sourceTitle: FAQs about AI - ANU Learning and Teaching
evidence.evidenceSnippet: ANU says Microsoft Copilot is available for all ANU staff and students.

evidence.sourceTitle: How to access and use Microsoft Copilot - ANU Learning and Teaching
evidence.evidenceSnippet: ANU says users should sign in with their ANU uID account to access Microsoft Copilot.

records.description: ANU officially provides Adobe Firefly as a generative AI image tool for text-to-image creation and image editing tasks.
records.howToObtain: Sign in at firefly.adobe.com using an ANU uID account; staff have enterprise access, and some students in specific courses/disciplines also have access.
records.costToUser: Free where ANU access is provided; staff are covered, while student access is limited to some courses/disciplines.

evidence.sourceTitle: FAQs about AI - ANU Learning and Teaching
evidence.evidenceSnippet: ANU says staff have Adobe Firefly access and some students from specific courses and disciplines have access.

evidence.sourceTitle: How to access and use Adobe Firefly - ANU Learning and Teaching
evidence.evidenceSnippet: ANU describes Adobe Firefly as a generative AI image tool.

evidence.sourceTitle: Guide for students: best practice when using Generative AI | Australian National University
evidence.evidenceSnippet: ANU guidance says Adobe Firefly is available for ANU staff and students through enterprise licences.

records.universityName: The Chinese University of Hong Kong (CUHK)
records.rawToolName: CUHK AI Chatbot for Teaching Support
records.description: CUHK-operated chatbot under the Pilot ChatGPT (& AI) Service for teaching support, with ChatGPT-based chat and image generation.
records.howToObtain: Apply through the CUHK web form linked on the service page; justification and use cases are required.
records.costToUser: Not stated as free; departmental/faculty/unit over-quota usage may incur charges.

evidence.sourceTitle: Pilot ChatGPT (& AI) Service for Teaching and Learning | CUHK EdTech
evidence.evidenceSnippet: CUHK AI Chatbot for Teaching Support is for teachers, TAs, and RPg students; prior application is required and access is restricted to CUHK network.

evidence.sourceTitle: Pilot ChatGPT (& AI) Service for Teaching and Learning | CUHK EdTech
evidence.evidenceSnippet: The current version says the ChatGPT chatbot employs the gpt-3.5-turbo model and its image generation is under the DALL-E shortcut.

evidence.sourceTitle: Pilot ChatGPT (& AI) Service Application Form for Teaching Support
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Official CUHK application form says the form is only for CUHK members.

records.universityName: The Chinese University of Hong Kong (CUHK)
records.rawToolName: CUHK AI Chatbot for Learning Support
records.description: CUHK-operated chatbot service for student learning activities under teacher approval within the Pilot ChatGPT (& AI) Service.
records.howToObtain: Teachers or TAs request enablement for classes via the linked CUHK application form; individual students need teacher approval.

evidence.sourceTitle: Pilot ChatGPT (& AI) Service for Teaching and Learning | CUHK EdTech
evidence.evidenceSnippet: The page names CUHK AI Chatbot for Learning Support for students approved by teachers.

evidence.sourceTitle: Pilot ChatGPT (& AI) Service for Teaching and Learning | CUHK EdTech
evidence.evidenceSnippet: It says teachers and TAs can apply to enable the service for classes, and applications from individual students will only be considered with teachers' approval.

records.universityName: The Chinese University of Hong Kong (CUHK)
records.description: Browser-based AI chat service with CUHK sign-in and enterprise data protection guidance.
records.howToObtain: Sign in through CUHK Microsoft 365 in a web browser with CUHK credentials and DUO.

evidence.sourceTitle: Microsoft Copilot Chat (Basic) | CUHK Information Technology Services Centre
evidence.evidenceSnippet: CUHK states Microsoft Copilot Chat (Basic) is an AI-powered productivity tool and says it is available to all students and staff.

evidence.sourceTitle: Microsoft Copilot Chat (Basic) | CUHK Information Technology Services Centre
evidence.evidenceSnippet: CUHK lists the service charge as free, provided under current Microsoft 365 licenses, and gives browser sign-in steps via CUHK M365.

records.universityName: The Chinese University of Hong Kong (CUHK)
records.description: Paid Microsoft 365 Copilot service integrated with Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, Teams, and organizational data.
records.howToObtain: Apply through the CUHK departmental subscription/application route, then access through the CUHK Microsoft 365 portal.

evidence.sourceTitle: M365 Copilot (Premium) | CUHK Information Technology Services Centre
evidence.evidenceSnippet: CUHK says M365 Copilot (Premium) is an AI-powered productivity tool integrating with Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, Teams, and more.

evidence.sourceTitle: M365 Copilot (Premium) | CUHK Information Technology Services Centre
evidence.evidenceSnippet: The service is available to all staff, project accounts and departmental accounts and is charged; application required.

records.description: The University of Edinburgh AI platform and secure gateway to multiple generative AI models.
records.howToObtain: Access through the University ELM service; student guidance says all University of Edinburgh students have access.

evidence.sourceTitle: ELM | Computing | Information Services
evidence.evidenceSnippet: ELM is the University of Edinburgh's AI innovation platform, a central gateway providing safer access to Generative Artificial Intelligence.

evidence.sourceTitle: Using generative AI in your studies: guidelines for students | Computing | Information Services
evidence.evidenceSnippet: All University of Edinburgh students have free access to ELM, which offers a secure gateway to a range of generative AI models.

evidence.sourceTitle: Introduction to ELM | Computing | Information Services
evidence.evidenceSnippet: ELM can assist all staff and students and is free to use for all staff and students.

records.description: Official Edinburgh pages state students can access Microsoft Copilot through their University Microsoft account, and central IT documents available Copilot instances.
records.howToObtain: Use your University Microsoft account; central IT says Edge Copilot chat is available by signing into Microsoft Edge with University credentials.

evidence.sourceTitle: Technology and equipment | School of Mathematics Teaching
evidence.evidenceSnippet: All University of Edinburgh students can access Microsoft Copilot through their University Microsoft account.

evidence.sourceTitle: AI in Microsoft 365 | Computing | Information Services
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Currently, you can access the following Copilot instances: Copilot chat service within Edge.

evidence.sourceTitle: AI in Microsoft 365 | Computing | Information Services
evidence.evidenceSnippet: You can access this by signing into the Microsoft Edge browser with your University credentials.

records.description: Microsoft 365 Copilot is presented as the University-wide AI suite for learning, research, and professional work.
records.howToObtain: Receive access through the University Microsoft 365 Copilot rollout and associated training.
records.costToUser: Included via a University licence; no personal purchase is stated on the cited pages.

evidence.sourceTitle: Approved Tools | AI Hub | StaffNet | The University of Manchester
evidence.evidenceSnippet: 65,000 colleagues and students will benefit from the full Microsoft 365 Copilot suite.

evidence.sourceTitle: World-first AI partnership between The University of Manchester and Microsoft announced
evidence.evidenceSnippet: The University of Manchester becomes first university in the world to provide Microsoft 365 Copilot access and training to all students and staff.

evidence.sourceTitle: AI in Teaching and Learning Policy
evidence.evidenceSnippet: University of Manchester Microsoft 365 Copilot access: This is the University licence for Microsoft Copilot.

records.description: Copilot Chat is the University-approved conversational AI assistant for day-to-day work, accessible on the web or in Microsoft Teams.
records.howToObtain: Log in with a University account and access it via web browser or Microsoft Teams.
records.costToUser: Free for staff on managed computers; no personal purchase is indicated on the cited pages.

evidence.sourceTitle: Copilot | IT Services | The University of Manchester
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Copilot Chat is available to everyone at the University of Manchester.

evidence.sourceTitle: Approved Tools | AI Hub | StaffNet | The University of Manchester
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Copilot Chat is an AI-powered assistant available to everyone with a University account and accessible via a web browser or within Microsoft Teams.

evidence.sourceTitle: Using generative AI tools and resources | StaffNet | The University of Manchester
evidence.evidenceSnippet: CoPilot Chat is free and available to all University staff on managed computers.

records.description: NebulaONE is an AI toolbox in the University cloud for creating custom AI agents with governance and no-code tooling.
records.howToObtain: Request early access via the Nebula One Request Form mentioned on the AI Hub page.
records.costToUser: Not stated on the cited pages.

evidence.sourceTitle: Approved Tools | AI Hub | StaffNet | The University of Manchester
evidence.evidenceSnippet: NebulaONE is an AI toolbox built within UoM’s cloud environment. It uses market-leading models to help you create custom AI agents, no coding required.

evidence.sourceTitle: Approved Tools | AI Hub | StaffNet | The University of Manchester
evidence.evidenceSnippet: The University says it recently started onboarding early adopters to test the NebulaONE AI system.

evidence.sourceTitle: Sign up for Greater Manchester AI Day! | StaffNet | The University of Manchester
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Manchester describes nebulaONE as an AI platform built on Microsoft Azure.
